title: cells add: whole-array validation report + --dry-run
read_first: skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs:953-981 (handleCellsAdd), :2136-2179 (worker prune dry-run pattern), :1783-1788 (dry-run hard-error discipline)
action: Per cli-ergonomics D2 (decision 8ef2bae6) and plan.md ce-2. Twins byte-identical. (1) cells.mjs addCells: aggregate per-cell failures — wrap validateNewCell per cell, collect {id, problems[]} across the WHOLE array (regen obligations included), fold duplicate-id detection in; cycle check unchanged (whole-batch, pre-write); on any failure throw ONE combined refusal naming every failing cell and every problem; all-or-nothing write unchanged. addCell (single) keeps today's behavior. (2) bee.mjs handleCellsAdd + registry cells.add entry: add dry-run boolean property (worker-prune pattern): --dry-run runs the full aggregate validation (schema, regen obligations, duplicates, cycle) and returns {dry_run:true, ok, cells:[{id, ok, problems}]} with exit 0 when clean — persists NOTHING in either outcome; verbs without dry-run keep the hard-error discipline (bee.mjs:1783-1788). (3) Tests red-first and SCOPED: batch with two bad cells -> one refusal naming both, nothing written; clean-batch dry-run -> exit 0, cells/ dir unchanged; dirty-batch dry-run -> verdicts listed, nothing written. Then regen: onboard --apply, render_plugin, release_manifest --write, impact_registry --write.

truths: a multi-cell payload never needs re-sending to discover the next error
regen complete: ledger parity, release manifest, impact registry green

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_cells.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check
